misconceptionSummary: Students think more decimal digits means a larger number.

visuals.ruleText: Decimal length and decimal size are different things

steps.narration: A longer decimal is not a larger decimal. The value depends entirely on which digits sit in which columns — not on how many digits there are.
Nine tenths is a larger column than eight tenths. The extra five hundredths in 0.85 can never make up the gap — a whole tenth separates them.
Padding with a trailing zero makes the comparison obvious. Ninety hundredths is more than eighty-five hundredths.

visuals.label: The tenths column decides — length is irrelevant

steps.narration: Zero point nine is greater. One digit after the decimal, but it is in the most important position.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: assuming 0.85 > 0.9 because 85 > 9. Compare by column, not by digit count.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: More digits does not mean more value. Check the tenths column first — it carries the most weight.
